title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for layered undo with restore, reset, revert, and references and requires the smallest safe layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Undo Layer Classification · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G41 is a quiz in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

Which action demonstrates Gita Undo Layer Classification without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Undo Recovery Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G48 is a quiz in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
